sections.title: A Strange Event
Talking to Gordon
Talking to Gordon again before starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. How are things?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hello there. Not too bad, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great to hear. All the best.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Thank you friend. See you later.

sections.title: Talking to Gordon again after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hello again. Any luck finding that beast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been trying, but I haven't made much progress yet.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well it can't be living too far from here. You might want to check with my wife, Mary. Perhaps she's seen something I haven't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: The name's Gordon. Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a nice farm you've got here.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Thank you. We built it ourselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? So you haven't been here long?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Nope. Not too long ago you couldn't safely travel around these parts. Too many lizardmen. But then some Hosidius explorers discovered how fertile this land was.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Lord Hosidius made a deal with Lord Shayzien and the lizardmen were cleared out to make room for the Farming Guild.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: When we heard, we decided to come up here and start up our own farm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Need a hand with anything?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: I think we're good. Thank you for the offer though.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well actually, there is something we've been having a problem with.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Recently, some strange beast has been stealing our livestock at night. The soldiers are too busy dealing with the lizardmen to help, so we haven't been able to stop it.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: You look like you know your way around a weapon. If you can kill the beast and bring me its head as proof, I'll ensure you are compensated for your trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bring back the head? I can do that. I'm good at killing things. Do you know where the beast is?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well it can't be living too far from here, but I don't know exactly where. You might want to check with my wife, Mary. Perhaps she's seen something I haven't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. I'll return once I have the head of this beast!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not something I can help with I'm afraid. Good luck though.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: I understand. I'm sure someone else will be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very nice. Well I hope it goes well for you.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Thank you friend. See you later.

sections.title: Talking to Mary
Talking to Mary again

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. How goes the headhunt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still trying to come up with a way to cover the beast in flour.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Well I'm sure you'll think of something. Just remember to use your head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Are you Mary?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: That's right. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [player name]. Your husband told me that some beast has been causing you both trouble. I've agreed to kill it and bring back its head!

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Ah, that's good to hear. We've already lost a lot of livestock to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well fear not. I'm sure I'll make short work of it. I do have one issue, however.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't yet know where to find this beast. I was wondering if you'd seen anything?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I'm afraid not. It only ever comes in the night, so we haven't been able to get a proper look at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. This is a problem.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Well let's see now...

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh! I have an idea! If this beast ended up getting coated in flour, it wouldn't be hard for you to track it back to its home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how would I get it coated in flour?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh come on now! You're the adventurer here! Why don't you use your head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Use my head? Wait... Did you just...?

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: I don't know what you mean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

sections.title: Talking to the Sergeant

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Do you know anything about the beast causing trouble around here?

lines.speaker: Sergeant
lines.text: The farmers mentioned it. We haven't seen anything though. Our focus is on the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely you'd notice a big beast wondering around though?

lines.speaker: Sergeant
lines.text: The farmers said it came at night. We're not on shift then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But then who's guarding the farm at night?

lines.speaker: Sergeant
lines.text: That's not our problem. We're just here to follow orders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I'll find the beast myself.

sections.title: Talking to Gordon after talking to Mary

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hello again. Any luck finding that beast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been trying, but I haven't made much progress yet.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Anything you need from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just one question. You said this beast has been going for your livestock. Which animals has it been targeting?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: It's mainly been going for the cattle. I fixed up a new gate which has kept it out the past couple of nights. Not sure how long it will hold though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

sections.title: Pouring the flour on the gate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There we go. Anything that touches this gate will end up coated in flour. Now just to wait for the beast to come...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like the beast tried to get in again. Now to see if the flour plan worked...

sections.title: A Strange Beast
Talking to Gordon after pouring the flour

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hello again. Any luck finding that beast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your wife gave me a good idea to help track it down. I'll have its head soon enough!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Great to hear!

sections.title: Talking to Mary after pouring the flour

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. How goes the headhunt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The flour idea worked great. Now I just need to find the beast's home.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Well keep your head up. I'm sure you'll manage it.

sections.title: Upon killing the Headless Beast

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... a headless beast. Can't really bring back the head if there isn't one. Let's hope Gordon is still happy to reward me...

sections.title: Talking to Mary after killing the beast but before talking to Gordon

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. How goes the headhunt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've run into a little issue. The beast is dead, but it didn't have a head.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh dear. You should probably talk to Gordon about this.

sections.title: Talking to Gordon after killing the Headless Beast
Talking to Gordon again after killing the Headless Beast

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hello again. Do you have the head of that beast yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well I can't pay you if you don't have the head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hello again. Any luck finding that beast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The beast is dead!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Wonderful! Do you have the head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's the thing...

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Is there a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it didn't actually have a head.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Didn't have a head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Like... at all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not even a proper stump.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I get paid now?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: I'm sorry, but no. I agreed to pay you for a head and you don't have one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well actually I do have a head. Quite a good looking one in fact. It was the beast that didn't.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: That's not what I meant. Look, it's nothing personal, but I can't be seen paying someone if they didn't do the job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I did the job... How am I meant to bring you the head of a beast with no head?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Maybe you killed the wrong beast? It doesn't make sense that a headless beast would be taking cattle. How would it eat them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know...

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well if you manage to get hold of the beast's head, let me know. Until then, I can't pay you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

sections.title: Using items on Gordon
Neilan's journal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have the head of the beast. I do have proof it was headless though.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Proof? This doesn't seem like proof to me. Anyone could have written this. Maybe you wrote it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You assume I can read and write.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Can you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well.. yes. I promise I didn't write this though.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well you would say that. I'm sorry but I'm not having this. Come back when you have the head of the beast.

sections.title: Big bones

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have the head of the beast. I do have the bones though!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Bones? I don't want bones! I want the beast's head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you considered that you might be being too picky?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Of course not! Besides, these bones could be from anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh! Fine!

sections.title: Ensouled bear head

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hmmm... I'm not so sure...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is literally the head of a dead bear. What is wrong with you?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: I can see this is the head of a bear! This beast we're dealing with is more than a mere bear though!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? You said you'd never seen the beast! How do you know it wasn't just a bear?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: We've dealt with bears before! This is clearly something more! Now, go and find the real beast!

sections.title: Bearhead

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: What? But this is clearly just part of a costume!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... No it's not...

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: It's hollow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe it didn't have any brains.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: The only thing without brains is you! Come back once you have the actual head!

sections.title: A Strange Fake
Talking to Mary after killing the beast
Talking to Mary again after killing the beast

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Keep at it! I've tried my hand at clay modelling in the past. You can make all sorts of things. Just get some soft clay and a knife and I'm sure you'll be able to make a lovely head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a shot!

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. How goes the headhunt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've run into a little issue. The beast is dead, but it didn't have a head. Your husband says he won't pay me until I bring him one.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh dear. I'm afraid Gordon can be quite picky sometimes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what do I do now? I can't bring him a head if there isn't one.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Well, between you and me, Gordon is a good farmer but I wouldn't say he's a genius by any means. He also doesn't actually know what the beast looks like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I make him a fake head! Great idea!

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: That's the spirit! I've tried my hand at clay modelling in the past. You can make all sorts of things. Just get some soft clay and a knife and I'm sure you'll be able to make a lovely head.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a shot!

sections.title: Making the clay head

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks awful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still, maybe it will be enough to convince Gordon.

sections.title: Talking to Mary after making the clay head

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made a head out of clay.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Wonderful! You should show it to Gordon.

sections.title: Using the clay head on Gordon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof!

sections.title: Talking to Gordon after making the clay head
Talking to Gordon again after making the clay head

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Do you have the head of that beast yet? Or do you just have more clay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have it yet.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: No head, no pay.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hello again. Do you have the head of that beast yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Its head? This is its head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: But this just looks like clay... It feels like clay...

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: It's falling apart... What creature is this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes! A good question! A very good question! A good question in need of a good answer I'm sure!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Okay... So what is the answer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... well... This is the head of a... a claybear!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Claybear? There's no such thing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're quite rare around here.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Quite rare because they don't exist! I'm no fool, this is clearly a pretend head! It doesn't even have any fur!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Come back once you have the real head of the beast!

sections.title: Talking to Mary after showing Gordon the clay head

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made a head out of clay. Gordon didn't buy it though.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh dear. Keep trying. I'm sure with a few tweaks you can make a head that will convince him.

sections.title: Making the fur head

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It somehow looks even worse than before!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gordon said it needed fur though. Maybe he'll be happy with this one.

sections.title: Talking to Mary after making the fur head

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made a head out of clay. Gordon didn't buy it though, so I've now added some fur.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Wonderful! You should show it to Gordon.

sections.title: Using the fur head on Gordon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof! For real this time!

sections.title: Talking to Gordon after making the fur head
Talking to Gordon again after making the fur head

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Do you have the head of that beast yet? Or do you just have more fakes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have it yet.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: No head, no reward. Come back once you have it!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Do you have the head of that beast yet? Or do you just have more clay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof! For real this time!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well there's fur this time. It does look like an actual head, I have to admit.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Yes, I think this is the head of a beast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wonderful! So about my payment?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well hang on just a moment...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Where's the blood? If this is a fresh kill, the head should have blood on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... Yes... Well the thing about this head is that this is the head of a...

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A... bloodbear?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Bloodbear? What's a bloodbear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a rare type of bear. One that doesn't have any blood.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: It's called a bloodbear but it doesn't have blood? Did a fool name it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well actually the one that named it is quite the intellectual... Very cool as well... not that I know them or anything.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: You must take me for an idiot! This is some head you bought from a taxidermist or something. It's clearly not a fresh kill!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Come back once you have the real head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh*

sections.title: Talking to Mary after showing Gordon the fur head

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gordon didn't buy the clay head so I tried adding some fur. He still wasn't convinced though.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Well I'm sure you're not far off. Keep it up!

sections.title: Using a vial of blood or a blood pint on the fur head
Making the bloody head

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Now it looks bloody!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... it doesn't. It actually looks like someone tried to dye a fur covered clay monstrosity red.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still, maybe Gordon won't notice...

sections.title: Talking to Mary after making the bloody head

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gordon didn't buy the clay head so I tried adding some fur. He still wasn't convinced so I've dyed it red to make it look bloody.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Wonderful! I'm sure this will convince him. You should show him what you've got.

sections.title: Using the bloody head on Gordon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof... Again!

sections.title: Talking to Gordon after making the bloody head
Talking to Gordon again after making the bloody head

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Have you mounted the head of the beast in our home yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Well let me know once you have and I'll give you your reward.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Do you have the head of that beast yet? Or do you just have more fakes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bested the beast and I bring you its head as proof... Again!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: It's...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: It's wonderful! This is clearly the head of the beast! Well done adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hooray!

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: There's just one thing though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh for the love of-

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: A head like this deserves a place of honour! My friend, please could you mount it in our home?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh. Fine.

sections.title: Talking to Mary after showing Gordon the bloody head

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that head?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Gordon was happy with the head I made. In fact, he was so happy that he wants it mounting in your home.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Oh dear. Well if it keeps him happy, I suppose. There should be a good spot near the stairs.

sections.title: Mounting the bloody head

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What a work of art! I've really outdone myself this time! I guess I should let Gordon know.

sections.title: Talking to Mary after mounting the bloody head

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Well I have to say that mounted head looks awful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Mary
lines.text: Not to worry. If it keeps Gordon happy, so be it. Anyway, you should go and let him know it's all done.

sections.title: Talking to Gordon after mounting the bloody head

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: Have you mounted the head of the beast yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all done.

lines.speaker: Gordon
lines.text: That's great! I'd say it's about time for your reward. But first, let's have a look at this head...
